ogTitle: Chiswick vs Chittaway Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chiswick and Chittaway Point.
heading: Chiswick vs Chittaway Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chiswick, New South Wales and Chittaway Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chittaway Point has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Chiswick (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chiswick or Chittaway Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chiswick has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Chittaway Point scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chiswick scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Chittaway Point scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chiswick or Chittaway Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chittaway Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
